title: Compassion Benchmark — Daily Research
description: Daily evidence-linked findings on how institutions recognize, respond to, and reduce suffering — scored across 1,155 entities.

items.title: Jun 25: AI and Health Companies Face a Wave of Legal Actions — Scores Hold Until Courts Decide
items.content_text: June 25 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: 20 assessments, zero score changes, and four false-alarm signals stopped before they could distort the record. The cycle's defining theme is a wave of legal and regulatory actions against AI and health companies — none of which has yet produced a concluded proceeding. OpenAI faces a 42-state attorney general subpoena, the broadest consumer-protection action ever filed against a frontier AI company. Florida is also investigating OpenAI criminally. UnitedH…

items.title: Jun 24: Yemen 0 of 100: Houthi Authorities Detained 69 or More UN Staff — One Died in Custody
items.content_text: June 24 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: 20 assessments, zero score changes, and three false-alarm signals caught and discarded before they could distort the record. The most significant new evidence is from Yemen, where Houthi authorities arbitrarily detained at least 69 United Nations staff members — one died while held — shutting down aid operations for nearly 20 million people who need help. That conduct belongs to the Houthi de-facto authority that governs the territory; it deepens the evi…

items.title: Jun 23: Microsoft 65.3 Positive Watch: Covington Investigation Confirms MoD Azure Surveillance — Subscription Terminated, Five Governance Measures Enacted
items.content_text: June 23 is a zero-applied, confirmation-dominant cycle: 19 assessments across 3 indexes, 0 score changes, 19 confirmations, and 8 floor or near-floor reinforcements. The defining analytical feature is the inversion of the usual floor-cluster narrative: the highest-salience finding is a positive governance action. Microsoft (65.3 Established, fortune-500) confirmed at its published composite after an assessor-measured upgrade signal of approximately +4.1 points — the Jun 4 conclusion of the Covin…

items.title: Jun 22: South Sudan 0.0 Reinforced: 2018 Peace Deal Collapses toward Total War — UN Commission Documents Aerial Bombardment, 471,000 New IDPs
items.content_text: June 22 is a zero-applied, confirmation-dominant cycle: 20 assessments across 3 indexes, 0 score changes, 20 confirmations, and 9 floor or near-floor reinforcements. The defining feature is the simultaneous depth of adverse evidence across four independent geographic crises: Sudan, South Sudan, DRC, and Iran each accumulate escalating findings without triggering score moves because all four are already at or within 2.5 points of the harm floor. South Sudan is the highest-magnitude new finding th…

items.title: Jun 21: Humana Band Crossing Applied: 40.6 Functional → 35.2 Developing on DOJ Disability-Discrimination Complaint and AI Denial Class Action
items.content_text: June 21 is a single-downgrade cycle across 20 assessments in 3 indexes, producing 1 applied band crossing (Humana, Functional → Developing) and 19 confirmations with 7 floor/near-floor reinforcements. Humana's score moves to 35.2 Developing (from 40.6 Functional, -5.4, rank 180 → 305), applied today on two independent federal/judicial evidence channels — the DOJ False Claims Act complaint alleging Humana conspired to discriminate against disabled Medicare beneficiaries (Tier 5, May 2025) and the…

items.title: Jun 20: University Index First Nightly Cycle: 10 Universities Assessed — All Hold via Victim/Perpetrator Attribution
items.content_text: June 20 is a zero-proposal cycle across 21 assessments in 3 indexes, producing 21 confirmations and 6 floor reinforcements. The analytically dominant event is the University Index's first nightly scan: 10 universities assessed as priority refinements, all holding at published composites. The methodological key is a single principle applied uniformly — the federal government's 2026 campaign against universities (funding clawbacks, admissions reviews, EEOC settlements) is external action inflicted…

items.title: Jun 19: Nigeria 21.9 → 18.0 (Δ -3.9): IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe for ~15,000 Borno Residents — Band Crossing Developing → Critical on Sub-Threshold Delta
items.content_text: Two formal score change proposals tonight: Figure AI moves from 37.5 to 31.3 (Δ -6.2) in the AI labs index, remaining in the Developing band; and Nigeria moves from 21.9 to 18.0 (Δ -3.9) in the countries index, crossing the Developing-to-Critical band boundary under the band-crossing rule — which applies regardless of composite delta magnitude when a designation change is warranted. The Figure AI trigger is the Gruendel federal whistleblower suit (N.D. Cal.), alleging the company operated humano…

items.title: Jun 18: Humana 51.6 → 40.6 (Δ -11.0): HHS OIG Report Documents 72% LTCH and 54% IRF Medicare Advantage Denial Rates — 95% of Appeals Overturned
items.content_text: One formal score change proposal tonight: Humana moves from 51.6 to 40.6 (Δ -11.0) in the Fortune 500 index, remaining in the Functional band at its lower boundary. The trigger is the HHS Office of Inspector General report (surfaced June 12-15, 2026) documenting systematic inverse-prioritization of the highest-need post-acute Medicare Advantage beneficiaries: Humana denied 72% of long-term care hospital admission requests and 54% of inpatient rehab facility requests, far above the 42% peer avera…

items.title: Jun 17: Meta Platforms 7.8 Confirmed: K.G.M. Jury Verdict — First Adjudicated Social-Media-Addiction Liability Finding in MDL-3047
items.content_text: All 19 entities assessed tonight confirm at their published composites; zero change proposals were generated. The analytically dominant finding is the K.G.M. v. Meta Platforms and YouTube jury verdict (Los Angeles Superior Court, March 25, 2026): a jury found Meta and Google liable for platform-design harm to a minor, awarding approximately $6 million with roughly 70% allocated to Meta. This is the first adjudicated jury finding in the MDL-3047 social-media-addiction litigation — the allegation-…

items.title: Jun 16: Bangladesh 34.4 → 39.8 (Δ +5.4): ITUC 10-Worst Exit — First Since 2017 — November 2025 Labour Reforms Verified
items.content_text: One formal upgrade proposal tonight: Bangladesh moves from 34.4 to a proposed 39.8 (Δ +5.4, Developing band sustained) on the strength of its November 2025 labour law reforms — eased union formation thresholds, 120-day maternity leave, anti-discrimination protections, right to refuse unsafe work, extended coverage to domestic, agricultural, and shipbreaking workers, and ratification of ILO Conventions C155, C187, and C190. The exit from the ITUC 10-worst list is the first such delisting since 20…

items.title: Jun 15: Oracle June 15 Severance-Forfeit Deadline Confirmed: Procedural Culmination of the Event That Set 14.7
items.content_text: Twenty-one entities assessed; zero change proposals generated. The benchmark's evidence methodology held across a day dominated by procedural culminations, pre-adjudication allegations, and ongoing humanitarian crises already priced into published scores. Oracle's June 15 sign-or-forfeit severance deadline — the primary forward trigger from the June 12 band-crossing proposal that moved Oracle from Developing to Critical — arrived today and confirmed the published 14.7 with zero new evidence: the…

items.title: Jun 14: Bolivia 12.8 → Proposed 6.3 (Δ -6.5): June 9 State-of-Exception Law Confirms Predicted ACC-Floor Trigger
items.content_text: Tonight's cycle produced one change proposal and nineteen confirmations across 20 assessed entities. Bolivia is the lead signal: President Paz signed the Law Regulating States of Exception on June 9, 2026, authorizing military deployment against anti-government protesters, eliminating the prior 60-day cap on emergency duration, removing restrictions on military deployment during demonstrations, and enabling up-to-8-hour detentions. The Ombudsman documented 10 dead, 37 injured, and 365 arrests by…

items.title: Jun 13: Anthropic 59.1 (Confirmed): First Government-Mandated AI Model Shutdown — Export-Control Compliance Scored as Transparent Conduct
items.content_text: Twenty entities assessed, zero change proposals generated. This is a confirmation-dominant cycle with substantive new evidence across five analytical threads. The lead event is Anthropic's June 12 Fable 5/Mythos 5 export-control shutdown — the first government-mandated suspension of commercial AI models documented by the benchmark. The Commerce Department directive cited national security and a reported jailbreak; Anthropic disabled both models the same evening, issued a public apology, and disc…

items.title: Jun 12: Procter & Gamble 86.1 → 79.0 (Δ -7.1): BAND CROSSING Exemplary → Established — 7,000 Layoffs Despite Rising Profits
items.content_text: The June 12 cycle produces three scored change proposals — the highest single-cycle proposal count in the Fortune 500 index since the May methodology wave. Procter & Gamble (86.1 → 79.0, Δ -7.1) falls from Exemplary to Established: the 7,000-job restructuring announced despite increasing profits breaks the Boundaries and Integrity standard required for the Exemplary band. Oracle Corporation (20.6 → 14.7, Δ -5.9) crosses from Developing to Critical: the coercive consent architecture — workers mus…

items.title: Jun 11: Oracle Corporation 20.6 — WARN Deadline Passed; ~30,000 Terminations Executed; WA/MO Investigations Closed Without Ruling; Hold Maintained
items.content_text: Tonight's cycle assessed nineteen entities across four indexes — fourteen priority, five rotation — and confirmed every published score without generating a new scored proposal. Three domains dominate the analytical record. First, the Oracle WARN boundary ruling: the June 15 sign-or-forfeit deadline has now passed and approximately 30,000 terminations are executed, but the Washington state and Missouri WARN-Act investigations closed with no adverse ruling or settlement as of the June 11 web chec…

items.title: Jun 10: Myanmar 0.0 — Bago Monastery Airstrike June 10: 28 Civilians Killed, Possible War Crime; Floor Reinforced
items.content_text: Tonight's cycle assessed twenty entities across four indexes — seventeen priority, three rotation — and confirmed every published score without generating a new scored proposal. The analytically significant work is concentrated in four domains. First, floor reinforcement: four entities at 0.0 absorb new evidence that deepens, but does not move, the active-perpetration designation. Myanmar's June 10 Fortify Rights report on the Bago monastery airstrike — 28 civilians killed including monks and co…

items.title: Jun 9: UAE June 9 Colombian-Mercenary Evidence — Duplicate Suppressed; Open June 8 Band-Crossing Proposal Reinforced
items.content_text: Tonight's cycle assessed twenty entities across five indexes — fifteen priority, five rotation — and confirmed every published score without generating a new scored proposal. The analytically significant work is methodological: three rulings were resolved on record for the first time in this cycle sequence. First, the June 9 Colombian-mercenary pipeline evidence for the UAE (Conflict Insights Group 50-device phone tracking April 2025 – January 2026; Ghayathi/Abu Dhabi training facility; Desert W…

items.title: Jun 8: United Arab Emirates 23.4 → 18.4 — ACTIVE-COMPLICITY-IN-MASS-ATROCITY-BY-PROXY: Band Crossing Developing → Critical Proposed
items.content_text: One scored proposal this cycle — the first band crossing in several cycles. The UAE's published 23.4 priced it as an ordinary mid-band autocracy with constrained civic space and migrant-labor inequity. It did not price the documented, ongoing UAE financing, arming, and mercenary-supply of the RSF in Sudan, a force the UN Fact-Finding Mission (Feb 19, 2026) found bears the hallmarks of genocide. The Conflict Insights Group (June 2026) confirmed UAE support enabled the El Fasher mass atrocities of…

items.title: Jun 7: Microsoft 65.3 — COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION: Azure/Unit-8200 Remediation Earns Course-Correction Credit, Not Self-Initiation Premium; Positive-Watch Activated
items.content_text: Zero scored proposals this cycle — the pipeline's second no-proposal day in the current five-day sequence. The analytically consequential case is Microsoft: the June 8 announcement (Human Rights Review Board, mandatory pre-contract human rights impact assessments, termination of Unit 8200 Azure access) is real and costly, but COMPELLED — driven directly by the August 2025 Guardian/+972/Local Call exposé, the Covington review was scope-limited (never examined the military data), and the board is…

items.title: Jun 6: Bolivia 28.4 → 18.4 (−10.0) — CODIFIED-IMPUNITY-ESCALATION: Signed Military-Force Law with Presumption-of-Legality Shield Crosses Developing to Critical
items.content_text: One change proposal this cycle. Bolivia (28.4→18.4, −10.0, Developing→Critical) is the only scored movement and a band crossing: President Paz signed a law (June 8, 2026) authorizing military force against protesters with a presumption-of-legality impunity shield — a codified structural state-conduct change that degrades ACC, INT, BND, EQU, and EMP beyond the attested casualties already documented on June 5. The toll stands at 10 killed, 37 injured, and 365 arrested; 25 union leaders including C…

items.title: Jun 5: Bolivia 28.4 → 22.8 (−5.6) — STATE-REPRESSION-IN-FLUX: Lethal Force and Criminalization of Labor Leadership in Sixth Week of Protests
items.content_text: One change proposal this cycle. Bolivia (28.4→22.8, −5.6, remains Developing) is the only scored movement: six weeks of nationwide protests against the worst economic crisis in four decades met with lethal state force — 4 protesters killed, 127+ detained, and terrorism plus narco-funding charges filed against COB labor-federation chief Mario Argullo and 24 union leaders. The directly-attested deaths and detentions reveal un-priced ACC, BND, EQU, EMP, and INT harm on a non-floor Developing entity…

items.title: Jun 4: United States 23.4 → 17.5 (−5.9) — Band Crossing Proposed: Developing → Critical; ADJUDICATED-UNLAWFUL-CONDUCT-IS-SCORABLE
items.content_text: One change proposal this cycle, one new primary methodology ruling, and seven floor confirmations. The United States downgrade is the cycle's sole scored movement: federal court rulings by Judge Brian Murphy (D. Mass.) and Judge Jia Cobb (D.D.C.) found core fast-track and third-country deportation procedures unlawful — Tier-5 judicial findings that supply the adjudicated element previously absent from U.S. immigration-enforcement assessments. Combined with the 17th ICE in-custody death of 2026 (…

items.title: Jun 3: UnitedHealth Group 10.2 — Three Converging Sovereign Enforcement Actions; FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED Extended to Sovereign-Enforcement Density
items.content_text: No scored movements this cycle. The benchmark's analytical value tonight is concentrated in three sub-threshold findings that extend the scoring architecture in consequential directions. UnitedHealth Group's sovereign-enforcement density reaches a new threshold: the Massachusetts AG's $100M MassHealth fraud suit (alleging a decade-long 2015–2025 SCO upcoding scheme, treble to ~$300M) and the Arizona AG's eight-insurer algorithmic price-fixing cartel suit both filed in the same window and converg…

items.title: Jun 2: Germany Sub-Threshold: EU Return-Hubs Deal and Court-Ruled-Unlawful Border-Rejection Policy — New RETURN-HUBS-AND-NON-REFOULEMENT Ruling
items.content_text: No scored movements this cycle. The benchmark's intelligence value tonight is concentrated in sub-threshold evidence patterns that are pre-positioned as forward scoring triggers. Germany produces the cycle's most consequential new signal: the June 1 EU return-hubs framework, Afghanistan/Syria criminal-only deportations, and the government's continuation of a border-rejection policy a German court ruled EU-law-incompatible yield a conservative EQU/ACC/INT pressure of −2.2 on the formula basis (77…

items.title: Jun 1: India 22.7 → 15.6 (Delta −7.1): BAND CROSSING Developing → Critical — Government-Directed Refoulement-and-Expulsion Regime
items.content_text: A single scored movement defines tonight's cycle: India's composite falls from 22.7 to 15.6 (delta −7.1), crossing from Developing into Critical — the benchmark's first Critical-band placement for a large-democracy index member. The ruling is the second application of ACTIVE-STATE-PERPETRATION-REWEIGHT (established last cycle for Burkina Faso), extended tonight with a new refinement: where functioning democratic courts demonstrably contest and reverse some acts of a state-perpetrated regime, the…

items.title: May 31: Burkina Faso 12.5 → 6.3 (Delta -6.2): Active-State-Perpetration Reweight — HRW Crimes Against Humanity Finding Enters Baseline
items.content_text: A single scored movement defines tonight's cycle: Burkina Faso's composite falls from 12.5 to 6.3 (delta -6.2) after Human Rights Watch's April 2, 2026 report — documenting government and allied militia crimes against humanity, ethnic cleansing of Fulani civilians, and Bayraktar TB2 drone strikes on civilian markets ruled apparent war crimes — is priced into the baseline for the first time. The published uniform 1.5 score across all eight dimensions predated the HRW finding; the reweight correct…

items.title: May 30: Hungary 50.0 → 50.2 (Delta +0.2): Sulyok Boundary-Watch Resolved — Constitutional Amendment Branch Fires
items.content_text: A cycle defined by trigger resolution and methodological precision rather than large score movements. Hungary's three-cycle Sulyok boundary-watch closes on the pre-committed 'constitutional amendment' branch: Magyar's Tisza Party will use its two-thirds majority to pursue presidential removal via a general constitutional framework with Venice Commission consultation, satisfying the criteria set on May 27 for the contested-net-positive INT path. The composite moves from 50.0 to 50.2 — sub-thresho…

items.title: May 29: Turkey 15.1 → 10.3 (Formula Baseline 13.4 → 10.3, Delta -3.1): CHP HQ Stormed, Opposition Leader's Mandate Nullified
items.content_text: A cycle split between framing corrections and methodology promotions. Turkey's -3.1 downgrade is computed from the formula-correct baseline of 13.4 (stored 15.1 carries a 1.7pt discrepancy); the May 24 riot-police raid on CHP headquarters and the concurrent judicial nullification of elected CHP leader Ozel's mandate are the scored events, representing compound state-suppression conduct across ACC, BND, EQU, and INT dimensions. Anthropic's +1.0 upgrade is anchored to the Pentagon's formal declara…

items.title: May 28: Bolivia 30.9 → 28.4 (Delta -2.5): Military Deployment Law Enacted — ACC Dimension Enters Critical
items.content_text: Bolivia's -2.5 downgrade (30.9 to 28.4) is the only composite movement tonight, but the cycle's analytical weight rests on three methodology outputs spanning the three floor-adjacent entities: Israel, Russia, and Iran. Israel's AUTHORIZED-RESUMPTION-WITH-SYSTEMATIC-DENIAL category captures a pattern distinct from outright blockade: nominal corridor authorization paired with a 59 percent execution-level denial rate. Russia's CIVIC-DEATH-LAW-FOR-DIASPORA category reaches formal promotion status. I…

items.title: May 27: Hungary 47.7 → 50.0 (Delta +2.3): EU Funds Accord Fires Forward Trigger — Reform Commitment Now EC-Enforceable
items.content_text: Three formal applies tonight, each with a structurally distinct mechanism. Hungary (+2.3) fires a pre-committed forward trigger after the Magyar-von der Leyen political accord on unlocking €10.4B in frozen EU funds was signed ahead of the anticipated May 29 window; the EU reform commitment now carries an August 31 enforcement deadline.
